body_txt: A common device for a series opening or Establishing Character Moment . Things begin in a frantic rush which usually results in taking shortcuts, such as eating breakfast on the run. The classic image is of someone running down the street, dragging their backpack with one hand while trying to put on their jacket or sweater one-handed with the other, bumping into important people in a manner that gets the plot started. The trope serves as an easy way to make the protagonist sympathetic, by establishing them as an underdog and also showing that they do care what others think, since a person who does not care about the opinion of others wouldn't be frantically rushing to get to school to avoid being even more late than they already are. It thus serves as a good starting point for a series in which the protagonist starts out clumsy, incompetent, and lacking self-confidence, and then gradually blossoms over the course of the story. The most common, yet hilarious subversion of this trope is when the character remembers at the last second that it’s a weekend, making their efforts moot . In manga and anime , this is associated with the Shoujo (girls') demographic, where the original intent was to establish the protagonist as cute but clumsy , or give her a minor flaw (in Japan, there is a stigma against eating in public, hence the classic image of the main heroine running late to school with a slice of toast hanging out of her mouth ). It has been overused to the point of becoming a Discredited Trope ; Japanese "How to Become a Shoujo Manga-ka" books specifically advise against using this trope, and manga even lampshade how much of a cliche it is. It is often paired with Crash-Into Hello . On the other hand, given the above, this trope has become so strongly associated with the poorly executed silly Romantic Comedy that now it can often be found in the parody of the genre, especially during the dreams of the characters about the ideal romance. It's also Truth in Television , as any teenager/parent/college student will tell you. Compare Easing into the Adventure , Good Morning, Crono .
